title: helianthemum canum subsp. nebrodense
intro: Helianthemum canum subsp. nebrodense is a rare and endemic subspecies of the rockrose plant, also known as Helianthemum canum, which is native to Sardinia in Italy. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Helianthemum canum subsp. nebrodense include its ability to protect against oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as an infusion or tincture, and its flowers are used to make a tea or syrup that can be used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
